messages.content:

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_raw:
{
    "mode": "human",
    "topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
    "opinion": "As a creative person who's also observant of the world around me, I've developed my own perspective on managing fake content online. Living in Rome as an Italian woman has taught me about authenticity and tradition – from cooking pasta properly without adding too much water to ensuring that what we see on social media reflects real life.\n\nI believe tackling fake content starts with education and awareness. Since I only check social media occasionally, it's easy for misinformation to slip past my radar, but when I do engage online, especially during birdwatching sessions where I document nature through photography, authenticity matters greatly. It’s like comparing a photo of an eagle you really saw versus one from stock images; the former feels more genuine.\n\nMy primary school education and moderate conscientiousness mean that while I understand facts better than most people my age would expect (thanks to my father's PhD background), it doesn't always translate perfectly. But creativity helps here – when spotting fake posts or ads promising miracle cleaning products for Rome apartments but actually being scams, I imagine alternative scenarios where communities collaborate.\n\nPerhaps one approach could be promoting community-driven content platforms that allow people like myself with part-time employment to contribute meaningfully while verifying their work through peer review systems inspired by Islamic principles of truthfulness. This way, we maintain integrity without sacrificing our need for creative expression.",
    "reasoning_summary": "I started from my personal experiences in Rome as a creative individual who spends limited time on social media but observes its effects closely. I then considered how authenticity aligns with traditional values and how misinformation can be countered through education and community efforts, drawing parallels to my hobbies like photography and birdwatching where real experience is more valuable than imitation. I also reflected on the educational background influencing one's ability to discern truth versus myth.",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
As a creative person who's also observant of the world around me, I've developed my own perspective on managing fake content online. Living in Rome as an Italian woman has taught me about authenticity and tradition – from cooking pasta properly without adding too much water to ensuring that what we see on social media reflects real life.

I believe tackling fake content starts with education and awareness. Since I only check social media occasionally, it's easy for misinformation to slip past my radar, but when I do engage online, especially during birdwatching sessions where I document nature through photography, authenticity matters greatly. It’s like comparing a photo of an eagle you really saw versus one from stock images; the former feels more genuine.

My primary school education and moderate conscientiousness mean that while I understand facts better than most people my age would expect (thanks to my father's PhD background), it doesn't always translate perfectly. But creativity helps here – when spotting fake posts or ads promising miracle cleaning products for Rome apartments but actually being scams, I imagine alternative scenarios where communities collaborate.

Perhaps one approach could be promoting community-driven content platforms that allow people like myself with part-time employment to contribute meaningfully while verifying their work through peer review systems inspired by Islamic principles of truthfulness. This way, we maintain integrity without sacrificing our need for creative expression.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I started from my personal experiences in Rome as a creative individual who spends limited time on social media but observes its effects closely. I then considered how authenticity aligns with traditional values and how misinformation can be countered through education and community efforts, drawing parallels to my hobbies like photography and birdwatching where real experience is more valuable than imitation. I also reflected on the educational background influencing one's ability to discern truth versus myth.
response_parsed.tone: Observational yet creative; thoughtful without being overly formal or academic
citations: Experiences from primary school and father's PhD education
